column2: AC-01
column6: AC-07,API,P1,"New physical SIM card available with a valid, unregistered IMSI.
column7: HLR, BSS/OSS, and SMS Gateway systems are fully operational and integrated.","Subscriber inserts the new SIM card into a compatible mobile device.

column2: The network detects the SIM insertion.
The system initiates IMSI validation against the HLR.
Upon successful IMSI validation, the system proceeds with BSS/OSS provisioning (MSISDN assignment, service plan activation).
BSS/OSS provisioning completes successfully.

The system dispatches a Welcome SMS to the newly registered MSISDN.",The system detects the SIM and starts the 60-second provisioning timer (AC-01). IMSI is successfully validated. MSISDN and service plan are assigned in BSS/OSS within 60 seconds (AC-03). The SIM status transitions to ACTIVE only after successful BSS/OSS provisioning (AC-05). The end-to-end activation completes in under 60 seconds (AC-07). A Welcome SMS is delivered to the subscriber within 30 seconds of SIM activation (AC-04).,Sample data or mock values needed for execution.
AC-02,API,P1,"New physical SIM card available with an invalid IMSI (e.g., malformed, non-existent in HLR).
column3: HLR system is operational.",Subscriber inserts the SIM card with the invalid IMSI into a compatible mobile device.
column4: The network detects the SIM insertion.
column5: The system attempts IMSI validation against the HLR.,IMSI validation fails. The system returns error code 'SIM_REJECT_001' (AC-02). The SIM status does not transition to ACTIVE. An error is logged with the invalid IMSI and a timestamp.,Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column2: AC-08,API,P1,A SIM card with an IMSI that is already registered and active in the network is available.
column3: HLR system is operational.,Subscriber inserts the already registered SIM card into a compatible mobile device.
column4: The network detects the SIM insertion.
column5: The system attempts IMSI validation against the HLR.,IMSI validation identifies the IMSI as a duplicate. The registration attempt is rejected (AC-08). An error is logged indicating duplicate IMSI registration. The SIM status remains unchanged (not activated again).,Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column2: AC-06,API,P2,"New physical SIM card available with a valid, unregistered IMSI.
column4: BSS/OSS is configured to simulate a provisioning failure (e.g., service plan not found, database error).",Subscriber inserts the new SIM card.
column5: The system detects the SIM insertion and successfully validates the IMSI.
column7: BSS/OSS returns a provisioning failure message.,"The provisioning failure is detected. An OSS alert is triggered (AC-06). An error is logged with a timestamp and the IMSI, detailing the provisioning failure (AC-06). The SIM status does not transition to ACTIVE. No Welcome SMS is dispatched.",Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column1: TC-REQ001-005,Welcome SMS Delivery Failure Handling,TC-REQ001-005,REQ-001,API,P2,"Successful SIM activation and BSS/OSS provisioning completed.
column2: SMS Gateway is configured to simulate a delivery failure (e.g., subscriber device unreachable, SMSC error).",Complete steps for TC-REQ001-001 up to successful BSS/OSS provisioning.
column3: The system attempts to dispatch the Welcome SMS.
column4: The SMS Gateway reports a delivery failure.,The system logs the Welcome SMS delivery failure. The system attempts to retry sending the Welcome SMS (if retry mechanism is implemented). An operator alert is generated for persistent delivery failures. The SIM status remains ACTIVE (as provisioning was successful).,Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column1: TC-REQ001-006,HLR Unavailability During IMSI Validation,TC-REQ001-006,REQ-001,API,P1,"New physical SIM card available with a valid, unregistered IMSI.
column2: HLR system is simulated to be unavailable or respond with a timeout.",Subscriber inserts the new SIM card.
column3: The network detects the SIM insertion.
column4: The system attempts IMSI validation against the HLR.
column5: The HLR system does not respond or responds with an error indicating unavailability/timeout.,The system logs an error indicating HLR unavailability or timeout. The activation process is halted or put into a pending state. The SIM status does not transition to ACTIVE. No BSS/OSS provisioning or Welcome SMS dispatch occurs. An alert is triggered for the HLR system issue.,Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column1: TC-REQ001-007,Performance: Concurrent SIM Activations Under Load,TC-REQ001-007,REQ-001
column2: AC-07,API,P2,"All systems (HLR, BSS/OSS, SMS Gateway) are operational and configured for 'normal network load' (e.g., X concurrent activations per minute).
column3: A pool of valid, unregistered SIMs is available for testing.","Initiate X concurrent SIM activation requests within a specified time window (e.g., 100 activations in 1 minute).
column4: Monitor the end-to-end activation time for each request.
column5: Monitor system resource utilization (CPU, memory, network I/O) during the test.",All X SIM activations complete successfully. Each end-to-end activation completes in under 60 seconds (AC-07). Welcome SMS is delivered for each activation within 30 seconds. System resources remain within acceptable thresholds.,Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column1: TC-REQ001-008,SIM Status Consistency After Partial Provisioning Failure,TC-REQ001-008,REQ-001,API,P2,"New physical SIM card available with a valid, unregistered IMSI.
column2: HLR is operational.
column3: BSS provisioning is configured to succeed, but OSS provisioning is configured to fail.",Subscriber inserts the new SIM card.
column4: The system detects the SIM insertion and successfully validates the IMSI.
column7: OSS provisioning fails.,"The system detects the partial provisioning failure. A rollback mechanism is initiated to revert BSS changes, or a compensation logic ensures data consistency across systems. The SIM status does not transition to ACTIVE, or reverts to a non-active state if it temporarily changed. An error is logged, and an OSS alert is triggered.",Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column1: TC-REQ002-001,Data Usage Counter Update on Session End,TC-REQ002-001,REQ-002
column2: AC-01,API,P1,Subscriber has an active prepaid data plan with remaining data.
column3: PCRF system is operational.,"Subscriber initiates a data session (e.g., browses a webpage, streams a short video).
column4: Subscriber ends the data session (e.g., disconnects, closes application).
column5: PCRF processes the session data.",The PCRF updates the subscriber's data usage counter within 5 seconds of the session end (AC-01). The updated usage accurately reflects the data consumed during the session.,Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column2: AC-02,API,P2,Subscriber has an active prepaid data plan. Current data usage is below 80%.
column3: SMS Gateway is operational.,"Subscriber consumes data, causing their total usage to cross the 80% threshold.
column4: The PCRF detects the threshold breach.
column5: The PCRF triggers an SMS alert to the subscriber.",An SMS alert with the message 'You have used 80% of your data.' is sent to the subscriber's MSISDN within 60 seconds of the 80% threshold being crossed (AC-02). The subscriber's data speed remains at full speed.,Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column1: TC-REQ002-003,Data Cap Exhaustion and Throttling Enforcement,TC-REQ002-003,REQ-002
column2: AC-03
column3: AC-04,API,P1,Subscriber has an active prepaid data plan. Current data usage is below 100%.
column4: Policy Engine is operational.,"Subscriber consumes data, exhausting their data cap (reaches 100%).
column5: The PCRF detects data cap exhaustion.
column6: The PCRF instructs the Policy Engine to apply throttling.
column7: Subscriber attempts to access various internet services (e.g., browse a simple website, send a text message via an app, perform a DNS lookup).",The subscriber's data speed is throttled to ≤ 128 Kbps within 10 seconds of cap exhaustion (AC-03). An SMS alert 'Data exhausted. Throttled speed active.' is sent to the subscriber. The subscriber is not completely blocked; HTTP/DNS traffic remains reachable at the throttled speed (AC-04).,Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column2: AC-05,API,P2,Subscriber is currently throttled due to data cap exhaustion.,Subscriber applies a data top-up or renews their data plan.
column3: The system processes the credit application.
column4: The PCRF receives notification of the top-up/renewal.
column5: The PCRF instructs the Policy Engine to restore full data speed.
column6: Subscriber attempts to access internet services.,Full data speed is restored within 30 seconds of credit application (AC-05). An SMS Confirmation 'Your data has been renewed.' is sent to the subscriber.,Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column1: TC-REQ002-005,Prevent Duplicate 80% Threshold SMS in Same Session,TC-REQ002-005,REQ-002
column2: AC-06,API,P3,Subscriber has an active prepaid data plan.
column3: The 80% usage SMS has already been dispatched to the subscriber in the current data session.,"Subscriber continues to consume data after the 80% SMS was sent, but before reaching the 100% data cap.
column4: The PCRF continues to monitor data usage.",No duplicate 'You have used 80% of your data.' SMS alert is sent to the subscriber within the same data session (AC-06).,Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column1: TC-REQ002-006,Usage Counter Reset and Data Archiving at Plan Renewal,TC-REQ002-006,REQ-002
column2: AC-07,API,P2,Subscriber has an active prepaid data plan with a defined renewal date.
column3: Historic data usage exists for the current plan period.,The system reaches the subscriber's plan renewal date/time.
column4: The system processes the plan renewal.,"The subscriber's data usage counter is reset to zero (AC-07). Historic data usage for the previous plan period is archived in the CDR (Charging Data Records) (AC-07). The subscriber's data plan is renewed, and full speed is available (assuming the renewed plan includes data).",Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column1: TC-REQ002-007,PCRF Unavailability/Slow Update Handling,TC-REQ002-007,REQ-002,API,P1,"Subscriber has an active prepaid data plan.
column2: PCRF system is simulated to be unavailable or respond slowly (e.g., >5 seconds for usage update).",Subscriber initiates and ends a data session.
column3: The PCRF attempts to update the data usage counter.
column4: The PCRF fails to update or updates with significant delay.,"The system logs an error or warning indicating PCRF unavailability or slow response. The data usage counter is not updated in real-time, potentially leading to inaccurate usage tracking. Threshold notifications or throttling might be delayed or not triggered correctly. An alert is triggered for the PCRF system issue.",Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column1: TC-REQ002-008,SMS Gateway Failure for Threshold Alerts,TC-REQ002-008,REQ-002,API,P2,Subscriber crosses the 80% data usage threshold.
column2: SMS Gateway is configured to simulate a delivery failure for the alert.,"Subscriber consumes data, crossing the 80% usage threshold.
column3: The PCRF triggers an SMS alert.
column4: The SMS Gateway fails to deliver the SMS alert to the subscriber.",The system logs the SMS delivery failure for the threshold alert. The system attempts to retry sending the SMS (if retry mechanism is implemented). An operator alert is generated for persistent delivery failures. The subscriber's data speed remains at full speed (as it's only an 80% alert).,Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column1: TC-REQ002-009,Throttling Enforcement Accuracy and Basic Connectivity,TC-REQ002-009,REQ-002
column2: AC-03
column3: AC-04,API,P1,Subscriber is throttled to 128 Kbps due to data cap exhaustion.,"Subscriber attempts to download a small file (e.g., 1MB).
column4: Subscriber attempts to browse a simple, text-heavy website.
column5: Subscriber attempts to perform a DNS lookup.","The actual data throughput for the subscriber is consistently measured at ≤ 128 Kbps (AC-03). Basic services like web browsing and DNS lookups remain functional, albeit at a reduced speed (AC-04). No complete blocking of HTTP/DNS traffic occurs.",Sample data or mock values needed for execution.

column1: TC-REQ002-010,Handling Multiple Data Plans/Add-ons,TC-REQ002-010,REQ-002,API,P2,"Subscriber has an active base prepaid data plan and an active data add-on (e.g., social media pack, night data).
column2: Each plan/add-on has distinct usage limits and potentially different throttling rules.","Subscriber consumes data that falls under the add-on's specific usage category, exhausting the add-on data.
column3: Subscriber then consumes general data that falls under the base plan, exhausting the base plan data.
column4: Monitor data usage tracking and policy enforcement.","The PCRF correctly tracks usage for each data plan/add-on separately. Throttling rules are applied based on the exhaustion of the specific plan/add-on data, following defined priority rules (e.g., add-on exhausted first, then base plan). Appropriate SMS notifications are sent for each plan's thresholds/exhaustion.",Sample data or mock values needed for execution.
